mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on future cash flows and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy rates are falling on a monthly basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above their unconditional median when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month, or if the sign of the conditional return reverses for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent labor-market baseline signals weakening household income and lower future demand, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative after the unemployment rate breaks higher.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average conditional return is not below zero over a full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates and HOUST is simultaneously strengthening, investors infer broad demand resilience and better near-term corporate sales, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal portfolio does not outperform when both series rise together, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO combined with a rising CPIAUCSL indicates stagflationary supply stress, which compresses margins and raises discount-rate pressure, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous INDPRO weakness and CPIAUCSL acceleration do not coincide with below-zero 20-day SPX returns, or if a single-series model matches the interaction model's accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in GDPC1 relative to prior quarters signals a real demand slowdown that eventually depresses earnings expectations, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days after GDP growth turns lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect vanishes outside recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already high and CPIAUCSL stops accelerating, the market reads policy as near the end of a tightening cycle, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as rate-risk eases.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, disinflation episodes do not produce positive 5-day returns, or if the sign flips negative once FEDFUNDS is above the threshold for more than 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS indicates job creation and income growth, which supports consumer spending and earnings expectations, making S&P 500 returns positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly PAYEMS gains fail to coincide with above-zero 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the signal is dominated by post-recession rebounds only.

mechanism_narrative: A strong HOUST reading only matters when RSAFS is also rising, because housing activity then reflects genuine end-demand rather than inventory restocking; under that joint condition, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if elevated HOUST without RSAFS confirmation does not weaken the signal, or if the joint condition fails to improve 60-day forward returns versus HOUST alone.
